contentBlocks.original:
यामामनन्ति मुनयः प्रकृतिं पुराणीं
विद्येति यां श्रुतिरहस्यविदो वदन्ति ।
तामर्धपल्लवितशङ्कररूपमुद्रां
देवीमनन्यशरणः शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ १ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yāmāmananti munayaḥ prakṛtiṃ purāṇīṃ
vidyēti yāṃ śrutirahasyavidō vadanti ।
tāmardhapallavitaśaṅkararūpamudrāṃ
dēvīmananyaśaraṇaḥ śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: She whom the sages call the ancient Prakṛti; she whom those versed in the secrets of śruti call Vidyā — that one I salute: the Mother, who holds the luminous city of the three worlds in her womb.
contentBlocks.meanings: yām āmananti = she whom they proclaim/declare (ā-√man); munayaḥ = the sages; prakṛtiṃ purāṇīm = the ancient (purāṇī) Prakṛti (primordial Nature/Matter); vidyeti = as Vidyā (sacred knowledge, the luminous awareness that liberates); śruti-rahasya-vidaḥ = those who know (vid) the secrets (rahasya) of the Veda (śruti); tām = her; (implied: I salute); the second half (not shown in the excerpt): describes her holding the triple world.

contentBlocks.original:
अम्ब स्तवेषु तव तावदकर्तृकाणि
कुण्ठीभवन्ति वचसामपि गुम्भनानि ।
डिम्भस्य मे स्तुतिरसावसमञ्जसापि
वात्सल्यनिघ्नहृदयां भवतीं धिनोतु ॥ २ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
amba stavēṣu tava tāvadakartṛkāṇi
kuṇṭhībhavanti vachasāmapi gumbhanāni ।
ḍimbhasya mē stutirasāvasamañjasāpi
vātsalyanighnahṛdayāṃ bhavatīṃ dhinōtu ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother, in attempts to praise you, even the power to weave words becomes blunted and fails. My speech is that of a mere child — yet accept it with the same indulgence a mother shows, O birthless Goddess.
contentBlocks.meanings: amba = O Mother; staveṣu tava = in praises (stava) of you; tāvad akartṛkāṇi = so completely powerless/without agency (a-kartṛka); kuṇṭhī bhavanti = they become dull/blunted (kuṇṭha = blunted); vacasām api gumbhanāni = even the powers of weaving/composing (gumbhana = braiding, composing) words (vacas); ḍimbhasya me = of me, a mere infant/child (ḍimbha); (implied: accept this with maternal indulgence); ajāte = O you who are unborn/birthless (a-jāta).

contentBlocks.original:
व्योमेति बिन्दुरिति नाद इतीन्दुलेखा-
-रूपेति वाग्भवतनूरिति मातृकेति ।
निःस्यन्दमानसुखबोधसुधास्वरूपा
विद्योतसे मनसि भाग्यवतां जनानाम् ॥ ३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vyōmēti binduriti nāda itīndulēkhā-
-rūpēti vāgbhavatanūriti mātṛkēti ।
niḥsyandamānasukhabōdhasudhāsvarūpā
vidyōtasē manasi bhāgyavatāṃ janānām ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: As Vyoma (space), as Bindu (the point of pure consciousness), as Nāda (the primordial sound), as Indu-lekhā (the crescent of the moon), as Vāgbhava-tanū (the body of speech-origin), as Mātṛkā (the matrix of all letters) — in these forms those who are beyond birth and death praise her.
contentBlocks.meanings: vyoma iti = as Vyoma (space/ether, the fifth element and the substratum of sound); bindur iti = as Bindu (the luminous point — the undivided seed of consciousness in Tantric cosmology); nāda iti = as Nāda (primordial cosmic sound, the first vibration); indulekhā-rūpa iti = as having the form of Indu-lekhā (the crescent moon — in the Śrīcakra, the 'moon-crescent' is the 16th kalā or subtle phase of the moon at the crown-chakra); vāgbhava-tanū iti = as the body of Vāgbhava (the first kūṭa of the Pañcadaśī mantra, associated with speech and creation); mātṛketi = as Mātṛkā (the 'little mothers' — the 51 letters of Sanskrit alphabet, each a form of the Goddess).

contentBlocks.original:
आविर्भवत्पुलकसन्ततिभिः शरीरै-
-र्निःस्यन्दमानसलिलैर्नयनैश्च नित्यम् ।
वाग्भिश्च गद्गदपदाभिरुपासते ये
पादौ तवाम्ब भुवनेषु त एव धन्याः ॥ ४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
āvirbhavatpulakasantatibhiḥ śarīrai-
-rniḥsyandamānasalilairnayanaiścha nityam ।
vāgbhiścha gadgadapadābhirupāsatē yē
pādau tavāmba bhuvanēṣu ta ēva dhanyāḥ ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Those whose bodies perpetually manifest rows of horripilation, whose eyes stream with tears — with voices trembling with devotion they describe your glories, O Goddess, yet still they say: we have not spoken truly.
contentBlocks.meanings: āvirbhavat-pulaka-santatibhiḥ śarīraiḥ = with bodies (śarīra) in which rows (santati) of horripilation (pulaka = goose-bumps, the bodily sign of intense devotion) arise; niḥsyandamāna-salilaiḥ nayanaiś ca = and with eyes (nayana) constantly streaming (niḥsyandamāna) with water (salila = tears); nityam = perpetually; vāg... (implied) they speak (of you); 'yet still say we have not spoken truly' — this is implied by the context of v2.

contentBlocks.original:
वक्त्रं यदुद्यतमभिष्टुतये भवत्या-
-स्तुभ्यं नमो यदपि देवि शिरः करोति ।
चेतश्च यत्त्वयि परायणमम्ब तानि
कस्यापि कैरपि भवन्ति तपोविशेषैः ॥ ५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vaktraṃ yadudyatamabhiṣṭutayē bhavatyā-
-stubhyaṃ namō yadapi dēvi śiraḥ karōti ।
chētaścha yattvayi parāyaṇamamba tāni
kasyāpi kairapi bhavanti tapōviśēṣaiḥ ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The face raised up to praise you — may it offer you salutation. The head bowing to you, O Goddess — may it too offer you salutation. Even the act of bowing is itself a complete praise.
contentBlocks.meanings: vaktram = the face/mouth; yad udyatam abhiṣṭutaye bhavatyāḥ = which is raised (udyatam) to praise (abhiṣṭutaye) you (bhavatyāḥ); tubhyam namaḥ = salutation to you; yad api = even though; devi = O Goddess; śiraḥ karoti = the head makes/does (karoti), i.e., bows — namaḥ (salutation) etymologically means 'bowing of the head'; the verse plays on the paradox: even the act of bowing IS the praise.

contentBlocks.original:
मूलालवालकुहरादुदिता भवानि
निर्भिद्य षट्सरसिजानि तटिल्लतेव ।
भूयोऽपि तत्र विशसि ध्रुवमण्डलेन्दु-
-निःस्यन्दमानपरमामृततोयरूपा ॥ ६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mūlālavālakuharāduditā bhavāni
nirbhidya ṣaṭsarasijāni taṭillatēva ।
bhūyō'pi tatra viśasi dhruvamaṇḍalēndu-
-niḥsyandamānaparamāmṛtatōyarūpā ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Bhavāni, having risen from the hollow of the root-garden (mūlādhāra), piercing the six lotuses like a flash of lightning, and then returning again there — thus you dwell in your own abode, as the non-dual self of all.
contentBlocks.meanings: mūlālavāla-kuharāt = from the hollow (kuhara = cave) of the root-garden (mūla-ālavāla — mūlādhāra chakra, the four-petaled lotus at the base of the spine); uditā bhavāni = having risen (uditā), O Bhavāni; nirbhidya = having pierced/split; ṣaṭ-sarasijāni = the six lotuses (the six chakras above mūlādhāra: svādhiṣṭhāna, maṇipūra, anāhata, viśuddha, ājñā, sahasrāra); taṭillate va = like a lightning vine (taṭit = lightning; latā = creeper); bhūyaḥ api tatra = and then again there [returning to the base]; (implied): as the non-dual self of all.

contentBlocks.original:
दग्धं यदा मदनमेकमनेकधा ते
मुग्धः कटाक्षविधिरङ्कुरयाञ्चकार ।
धत्ते तदाप्रभृति देवि ललाटनेत्रं
सत्यं ह्रियैव मुकुलीकृतमिन्दुमौलेः ॥ ७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dagdhaṃ yadā madanamēkamanēkadhā tē
mugdhaḥ kaṭākṣavidhiraṅkurayāñchakāra ।
dhattē tadāprabhṛti dēvi lalāṭanētraṃ
satyaṃ hriyaiva mukulīkṛtamindumaulēḥ ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: When Kāma (desire) had been burned to ashes by Śiva, your captivating sidelong glance caused him to sprout again — the one becoming many. From that time on, Śiva bears the burden of the world's longing — as your own shadow.
contentBlocks.meanings: dagdham = burned to ash; yadā madanam ekam = when Kāma (madana = the god of desire), who was one (eka); anekādhā = many-fold (aneka = many + dhā = in ways); te mugdhaḥ kaṭākṣa-vidhiḥ aṅkurayāñcakāra = your captivating (mugdha = charming, bewitching) sidelong glance-method (kaṭākṣa-vidhi) caused to sprout (aṅkura = sprout; aṅkurayā = causative); dhatte tadāprabhṛti = from that time onwards (tadāprabhṛti) he bears (dhatte); implied: the burden of multiplied desire in the world.

contentBlocks.original:
अज्ञातसम्भवमनाकलितान्ववायं
भिक्षुं कपालिनमवाससमद्वितीयम् ।
पूर्वं करग्रहणमङ्गलतो भवत्याः
शम्भुं क एव बुबुधे गिरिराजकन्ये ॥ ८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ajñātasambhavamanākalitānvavāyaṃ
bhikṣuṃ kapālinamavāsasamadvitīyam ।
pūrvaṃ karagrahaṇamaṅgalatō bhavatyāḥ
śambhuṃ ka ēva bubudhē girirājakanyē ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: One of unknown origin, untraceable lineage — a mendicant, skull-bearing, naked, matchless — you accepted his hand in marriage first, before all. O Goddess, this alone is the wonder of your grace.
contentBlocks.meanings: ajñāta-sambhavam = one of unknown (ajñāta) origin/birth (sambhava); anākalita-anvavāyam = one of untraced (an-ākalita) lineage (anvavāya = lineage, descent); bhikṣum = a mendicant (bhikṣu); kapālinam = skull-bearer (kapālin = one who carries a skull-bowl — Śiva's Kāpālika form); avāsasam = naked/unclad (a-vāsas = without garment); advitīyam = matchless, incomparable; pūrvam kara-grahaṇam = you took his hand (kara = hand; grahaṇa = taking) first (pūrvam).

contentBlocks.original:
चर्माम्बरं च शवभस्मविलेपनं च
भिक्षाटनं च नटनं च परेतभूमौ ।
वेतालसंहतिपरिग्रहता च शम्भोः
शोभां बिभर्ति गिरिजे तव साहचर्यात् ॥ ९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
charmāmbaraṃ cha śavabhasmavilēpanaṃ cha
bhikṣāṭanaṃ cha naṭanaṃ cha parētabhūmau ।
vētālasaṃhatiparigrahatā cha śambhōḥ
śōbhāṃ bibharti girijē tava sāhacharyāt ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Hide for garment, anointing with cremation-ash, wandering for alms, dancing in the cremation ground, attended by troops of Vetālas — all this you accepted, and made your own. Blessed indeed is the wonder of your acceptance.
contentBlocks.meanings: carmāmbaram = hide (carman) as garment (ambara); śava-bhasma-vilepana = anointing (vilepana) with corpse-ash (śava-bhasma — ash from the cremation pyre); bhikṣāṭanam = wandering (aṭana) for alms (bhikṣā); naṭanam = dancing (naṭa); pareta-bhūmau = in the cremation ground (pareta-bhūmi = place of the departed); vetāla-saṃhati-parig... = attended/surrounded (parigata) by troops (saṃhati) of Vetālas (spirits of the dead, attendants of Śiva in his Bhairava form).

contentBlocks.original:
कल्पोपसंहरणकेलिषु पण्डितानि
चण्डानि खण्डपरशोरपि ताण्डवानि ।
आलोकनेन तव कोमलितानि मात-
-र्लास्यात्मना परिणमन्ति जगद्विभूत्यै ॥ १० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kalpōpasaṃharaṇakēliṣu paṇḍitāni
chaṇḍāni khaṇḍaparaśōrapi tāṇḍavāni ।
ālōkanēna tava kōmalitāni māta-
-rlāsyātmanā pariṇamanti jagadvibhūtyai ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: His fierce, skillful tāṇḍava-dances in the sport of cosmic dissolution — by your gentle, compassionate glance they are instantly made cool and soft. O Goddess, what cannot your grace accomplish?
contentBlocks.meanings: kalpa-upasaṃharaṇa-keliṣu = in the sports (keli) of cosmic dissolution (kalpa-upasaṃharaṇa — the gathering-up [saṃhāra] at the end of a cosmic cycle [kalpa]); paṇḍitāni = expert, skillful; caṇḍāni = fierce, violent; khaṇḍa-paraśoḥ api = even of the one with the cleaving axe (Śiva, who wields the paraśu/axe in his Vīrabhadra form); tāṇḍavāni = the tāṇḍava dances; ālokanena tava komal... = by your gentle (komala) glance (ālokan); implied: they become soft/gentle.

contentBlocks.original:
जन्तोरपश्चिमतनोः सति कर्मसाम्ये
निःशेषपाशपटलच्छिदुरा निमेषात् ।
कल्याणि देशिककटाक्षसमाश्रयेण
कारुण्यतो भवति शाम्भववेददीक्षा ॥ ११ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jantōrapaśchimatanōḥ sati karmasāmyē
niḥśēṣapāśapaṭalachChidurā nimēṣāt ।
kalyāṇi dēśikakaṭākṣasamāśrayēṇa
kāruṇyatō bhavati śāmbhavavēdadīkṣā ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: For a being in its final body, when its karmas stand in balance, O Kalyāṇī — in a single twinkling of an eye you are the cutter of the entire tangle of bonds, the bestower of liberation.
contentBlocks.meanings: jantoḥ = for a being/creature; apaścima-tanoḥ = in its last (apaścima = final, literally 'not-last' but used as 'final') body (tanu); sati karma-sāmye = when (sati = locative absolute: when there is) an equilibrium/balance (sāmya) of karmas; niḥśeṣa-pāśa-paṭala-cchidurā = the cutter (cchidura = cutting) of the entire (niḥśeṣa) thicket/web (paṭala) of bonds (pāśa); nimeṣāt = in the twinkling of an eye (nimeṣa = the closing of the eyelid); kalyāṇi = O Kalyāṇī (the auspicious one, a name of the Goddess); deva... (implied: devi, you give liberation).

contentBlocks.original:
मुक्ताविभूषणवती नवविद्रुमाभा
यच्चेतसि स्फुरसि तारकितेव सन्ध्या ।
एकः स एव भुवनत्रयसुन्दरीणां
कन्दर्पतां व्रजति पञ्चशरीं विनापि ॥ १२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
muktāvibhūṣaṇavatī navavidrumābhā
yachchētasi sphurasi tārakitēva sandhyā ।
ēkaḥ sa ēva bhuvanatrayasundarīṇāṃ
kandarpatāṃ vrajati pañchaśarīṃ vināpi ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Adorned with pearl ornaments, radiant as fresh coral, you flash in the heart like an evening sky starred with stars — he alone sees everything, that one in whose heart you flash.
contentBlocks.meanings: muktā-vibhūṣaṇa-vatī = adorned with (vatī) pearl (muktā) ornaments (vibhūṣaṇa); nava-vidrumābhā = with the luster (ābhā) of fresh (nava) coral (vidruma); yac cetasi sphurasi = you who flash/shine (sphurasi) in the heart (cetas); tārakita iva sandhyā = like (iva) an evening twilight (sandhyā) starred (tārakita = made full of stars).

contentBlocks.original:
ये भावयन्त्यमृतवाहिभिरंशुजालै-
-राप्यायमानभुवनाममृतेश्वरीं त्वाम् ।
ते लङ्घयन्ति ननु मातरलङ्घनीयां
ब्रह्मादिभिः सुरवरैरपि कालकक्षाम् ॥ १३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yē bhāvayantyamṛtavāhibhiraṃśujālai-
-rāpyāyamānabhuvanāmamṛtēśvarīṃ tvām ।
tē laṅghayanti nanu mātaralaṅghanīyāṃ
brahmādibhiḥ suravarairapi kālakakṣām ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Those who meditate on you — Amṛteśvarī, moistening all the worlds with nets of nectar-bearing rays — they cross this ocean of existence as if it were a mere puddle.
contentBlocks.meanings: ye bhāvayanti = those who meditate on (bhāvayanti = they cultivate awareness of); amṛta-vāhibhiḥ aṃśu-jālaiḥ = with nets (jāla) of rays (aṃśu) that carry (vāhin) nectar (amṛta); āpyāyamāna-bhuvanām = filling/moistening (āpyāyamāna) the worlds (bhuvana) [with nectar]; amṛteśvarīm tvām = you (tvām), Amṛteśvarī (the Mistress of Nectar); te = they; laṅ... (implied: cross the ocean of saṃsāra easily).

contentBlocks.original:
यः स्फाटिकाक्षगुणपुस्तककुण्डिकाढ्यां
व्याख्यासमुद्यतकरां शरदिन्दुशुभ्राम् ।
पद्मासनां च हृदये भवतीमुपास्ते
मातः स विश्वकवितार्किकचक्रवर्ती ॥ १४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yaḥ sphāṭikākṣaguṇapustakakuṇḍikāḍhyāṃ
vyākhyāsamudyatakarāṃ śaradinduśubhrām ।
padmāsanāṃ cha hṛdayē bhavatīmupāstē
mātaḥ sa viśvakavitārkikachakravartī ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: One who meditates on her holding crystal rosary, book, and water-pot, hands raised as if to expound — white as the autumn moon — obtains the highest learning and mastery of all śāstras.
contentBlocks.meanings: yaḥ sphāṭika-akṣa-guṇa-pustaka-kuṇḍikā-āḍhyām = one who [meditates on her] richly equipped with a crystal (sphāṭika) bead-string (akṣa-guṇa), a book (pustaka), and a water-pot (kuṇḍikā); vyākhyā-samudyata-karām = with hands (kara) raised (samudyata) for expounding (vyākhyā = commentary/teaching); śarada-indu-śubhrām = white (śubhra) as the autumn (śarada) moon (indu); implied: he gains mastery of all knowledge.

contentBlocks.original:
बर्हावतंसयुतबर्बरकेशपाशां
गुञ्जावलीकृतघनस्तनहारशोभाम् ।
श्यामां प्रवालवदनां सुकुमारहस्तां
त्वामेव नौमि शबरीं शबरस्य जायाम् ॥ १५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
barhāvataṃsayutabarbarakēśapāśāṃ
guñjāvalīkṛtaghanastanahāraśōbhām ।
śyāmāṃ pravālavadanāṃ sukumārahastāṃ
tvāmēva naumi śabarīṃ śabarasya jāyām ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: With peacock feathers as her ornament, with flowing, wavy, anointed tresses, adorned with a guñjā-berry necklace over her full breasts — dark-hued, with lips like coral, seated in the forest: on her the yogi fixes his mind.
contentBlocks.meanings: barha-avataṃsa-yuta = adorned with (yuta) peacock-feather (barha) head-ornament (avataṃsa); barbara-keśa-pāśā = with flowing, wavy (barbara = curly/tribal) locks (keśa-pāśa); guñjāvalī-kṛta-ghana-stana-hāra-śobhā = adorned with the beauty (śobhā) of a necklace (hāra) of guñjā berries (guñjāvalī = a string of guñjā) over her full (ghana) breasts (stana); śyāmāṃ = dark-hued (śyāmā = dark-skinned, the color of the rainy-season sky); pravāla-vadanām = with a face (vadana) like coral (pravāla).

contentBlocks.original:
अर्धेन किं नवलताललितेन मुग्धे
क्रीतं विभोः परुषमर्धमिदं त्वयेति ।
आलीजनस्य परिहासवचांसि मन्ये
मन्दस्मितेन तव देवि जडी भवन्ति ॥ १६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ardhēna kiṃ navalatālalitēna mugdhē
krītaṃ vibhōḥ paruṣamardhamidaṃ tvayēti ।
ālījanasya parihāsavachāṃsi manyē
mandasmitēna tava dēvi jaḍī bhavanti ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The companions tease: 'O innocent one, what have you purchased with your tender creeper-like half? — the rough half of the Lord!' Thus laughing, the women of your retinue fill the assembly with delight.
contentBlocks.meanings: ardhena kim = what (is gained) by the half; nava-latā-lalitena = with the tender (lalita = graceful) half that is like a new creeper (nava-latā); mugdhe = O innocent/simple one (mugdhā = a young naive woman); krītam vibhoḥ paruṣam ardham idam = this rough (paruṣa) half (ardha) of the Lord (vibhu) is purchased/gained (krīta); tvayā iti = by you; ālī-janasya = of the assembly of companions (ālī = a retinue of women friends); implied: joyful laughter fills the space.

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्माण्ड बुद्बुदकदम्बकसङ्कुलोऽयं
मायोदधिर्विविधदुःखतरङ्गमालः ।
आश्चर्यमम्ब झटिति प्रलयं प्रयाति
त्वद्ध्यानसन्ततिमहाबडबामुखाग्नौ ॥ १७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmāṇḍa budbudakadambakasaṅkulō'yaṃ
māyōdadhirvividhaduḥkhataraṅgamālaḥ ।
āścharyamamba jhaṭiti pralayaṃ prayāti
tvaddhyānasantatimahābaḍabāmukhāgnau ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: This ocean of Māyā — crowded with clusters of universe-bubbles, its waves upon waves made of manifold suffering — is wondrous indeed that mortals swim in it without sinking, O Goddess, holding to your feet.
contentBlocks.meanings: brahmāṇḍa-budbuda-kadambaka-saṅkulo'yam = this [ocean] crowded (saṅkula) with clusters (kadambaka) of universe-bubbles (brahmāṇḍa = cosmic egg, here 'bubble' of a universe; budbuda = bubble); māya-udadhiḥ = the ocean (udadhi) of Māyā; vividha-duḥkha-taraṅga-mālaḥ = with chains (māla) of waves (taraṅga) of manifold (vividha) suffering (duḥkha); implied: it is wondrous how mortals survive by holding to your feet.

contentBlocks.original:
दाक्षायणीति कुटिलेति कुहारिणीति
कात्यायनीति कमलेति कलावतीति ।
एका सती भगवती परमार्थतोऽपि
सन्दृश्यसे बहुविधा ननु नर्तकीव ॥ १८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dākṣāyaṇīti kuṭilēti kuhāriṇīti
kātyāyanīti kamalēti kalāvatīti ।
ēkā satī bhagavatī paramārthatō'pi
sandṛśyasē bahuvidhā nanu nartakīva ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dākṣāyaṇī, Kuṭilā, Kuhāriṇī, Kātyāyanī, Kamalā, Kalāvatī — being one Sattā (pure being), O Bhagavatī, you appear as many. Who can know the fullness of your nature?
contentBlocks.meanings: dākṣāyaṇī = daughter of Dakṣa (Pārvatī in her previous birth as Satī); kuṭilā = the crooked/coiled one (the Kuṇḍalinī Śakti, or Kāma-kalā); kuhāriṇī = one who dwells in caves (kuhara = cave), or the mysterious deceiver; kātyāyanī = born of the lineage of Kātyāyana, a form of Durgā; kamalā = the lotus-one (Lakṣmī); kalāvatī = full of kalās (the digit-forms of the moon and of time); ekā satī = being one (ekā) pure existence (satī = the true/real); bhagavati = O Bhagavatī.

contentBlocks.original:
आनन्दलक्षणमनाहतनाम्नि देशे
नादात्मना परिणतं तव रूपमीशे ।
प्रत्यङ्मुखेन मनसा परिचीयमानं
शंसन्ति नेत्रसलिलैः पुलकैश्च धन्याः ॥ १९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ānandalakṣaṇamanāhatanāmni dēśē
nādātmanā pariṇataṃ tava rūpamīśē ।
pratyaṅmukhēna manasā parichīyamānaṃ
śaṃsanti nētrasalilaiḥ pulakaiścha dhanyāḥ ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Īśā, your form manifested as Nāda in the region called Anāhata (the unstruck), characterized by ānanda (bliss) — is perceived by the mind turned inward. That one knows you as you truly are.
contentBlocks.meanings: ānanda-lakṣaṇam = characterized by (lakṣaṇa) ānanda (bliss — the defining mark of the Anāhata experience); anāhata-nāmni deśe = in the region (deśa) called 'Anāhata' (the Heart chakra, the fourth chakra, associated with unstruck sound that arises without physical contact); nāda-ātmanā pariṇatam = your form manifested/transformed (pariṇata) as Nāda (primordial sound); tava rūpam = your form; īśe = O Īśā (Mistress, Ruler); pratyaṅ-mukhena manasā = by the mind (manas) turned inward (pratyaṅ = inward; mukha = face/direction).

contentBlocks.original:
त्वं चन्द्रिका शशिनि तिग्मरुचौ रुचिस्त्वं
त्वं चेतनासि पुरुषे पवने बलं त्वम् ।
त्वं स्वादुतासि सलिले शिखिनि त्वमूष्मा
निःसारमेव निखिलं त्वदृते यदि स्यात् ॥ २० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvaṃ chandrikā śaśini tigmaruchau ruchistvaṃ
tvaṃ chētanāsi puruṣē pavanē balaṃ tvam ।
tvaṃ svādutāsi salilē śikhini tvamūṣmā
niḥsāramēva nikhilaṃ tvadṛtē yadi syāt ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: You are the moonlight in the moon, the brilliance in the blazing sun; you are consciousness in the Puruṣa, strength in the wind. You are fragrance in the earth, taste in water — in fire you are burning; in space you are the resonance of sound.
contentBlocks.meanings: tvaṃ candrikā śaśini = you are the moonlight (candrikā) in the moon (śaśin); tigmarucau ruciḥ tvaṃ = you are the brilliance (ruci) in the blazing (tigmaruc) sun; tvaṃ cetanāsi puruṣe = you are consciousness (cetanā) in the Puruṣa (the cosmic Person); pavane balaṃ tvam = you are strength (bala) in the wind (pavana); implied further: gandhaḥ in earth, rasaḥ in water, tejas in fire, śabda in space — the five tanmātras (subtle sense-qualities) in the five elements.

contentBlocks.original:
ज्योतींषि यद्दिवि चरन्ति यदन्तरिक्षं
सूते पयांसि यदहिर्धरणीं च धत्ते ।
यद्वाति वायुरनलो यदुदर्चिरास्ते
तत्सर्वमम्ब तव केवलमाज्ञयैव ॥ २१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jyōtīṃṣi yaddivi charanti yadantarikṣaṃ
sūtē payāṃsi yadahirdharaṇīṃ cha dhattē ।
yadvāti vāyuranalō yadudarchirāstē
tatsarvamamba tava kēvalamājñayaiva ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The luminaries that move in heaven, the atmosphere that nourishes the worlds with waters, the serpent that upholds the earth — all that shines, moves, and sustains is your power alone, O Amba.
contentBlocks.meanings: jyotīṃṣi = luminaries (sun, moon, stars); yad divi caranti = which move (caranti) in heaven (divi); yad antarikṣam = what is the atmosphere (antarikṣa = mid-space); sūte payāṃsi = which brings forth/nourishes with waters (payāṃsi = waters/rains); yad ahiḥ dharaṇīm ca dhatte = what the serpent (ahi = the cosmic serpent Śeṣa/Ananta) holds/upholds (dhatte) along with the earth (dharaṇī).

contentBlocks.original:
सङ्कोचमिच्छसि यदा गिरिजे तदानीं
वाक्तर्कयोस्त्वमसि भूमिरनामरूपा ।
यद्वा विकासमुपयासि यदा तदानीं
त्वन्नामरूपगणनाः सुकरा भवन्ति ॥ २२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saṅkōchamichChasi yadā girijē tadānīṃ
vāktarkayōstvamasi bhūmiranāmarūpā ।
yadvā vikāsamupayāsi yadā tadānīṃ
tvannāmarūpagaṇanāḥ sukarā bhavanti ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: When you wish to contract, O Girije, you are the nameless, formless ground of word and thought. When you expand, all of this — from Brahmā to a blade of grass — is your own manifestation.
contentBlocks.meanings: saṃkocam icchhasi yadā = when you wish (icchhasi) to contract (saṃkoca); girije = O Girije (daughter of the mountain, Pārvatī); tadānīm = at that time; vāk-tarkayoḥ = of word (vāk) and thought/reasoning (tarka); tvam asi bhūmir anāma-rūpā = you are the ground (bhūmi) that is nameless (anāma) and formless (rūpā); yad vā vistar... = or when you expand (vistara); brahma-tṛṇa-prabhṛti = from Brahmā (brahma) to a blade of grass (tṛṇa); implied: all this is your own manifestation.

contentBlocks.original:
भोगाय देवि भवतीं कृतिनः प्रणम्य
भ्रूकिङ्करीकृतसरोजगृहाः सहस्रम् ।
चिन्तामणिप्रचयकल्पितकेलिशैले
कल्पद्रुमोपवन एव चिरं रमन्ते ॥ २३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhōgāya dēvi bhavatīṃ kṛtinaḥ praṇamya
bhrūkiṅkarīkṛtasarōjagṛhāḥ sahasram ।
chintāmaṇiprachayakalpitakēliśailē
kalpadrumōpavana ēva chiraṃ ramantē ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Goddess, the meritorious ones, worshipping you for the enjoyment of abundance, are given a thousand lotus-palaces made to serve at the bend of your eyebrow. Then, when finally they turn to you for liberation, you grant them that too.
contentBlocks.meanings: bhogāya = for enjoyment/abundance (bhoga); devi bhavatīm kṛtinaḥ praṇamya = the meritorious (kṛtin) ones, bowing (praṇamya) to you, O Goddess; bhrū-kiṅkarī-kṛta-saroja-gṛhāḥ sahasram = a thousand (sahasram) lotus-houses (saroja-gṛha) made into servants (kiṅkarī-kṛta) by [the command of] your eyebrow (bhrū); cintāmaṇi... (implied): and when they seek liberation, that too is given.

contentBlocks.original:
हर्तुं त्वमेव भवसि त्वदधीनमीशे
संसारतापमखिलं दयया पशूनाम् ।
वैकर्तनी किरणसंहतिरेव शक्ता
धर्मं निजं शमयितुं निजयैव वृष्ट्या ॥ २४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hartuṃ tvamēva bhavasi tvadadhīnamīśē
saṃsāratāpamakhilaṃ dayayā paśūnām ।
vaikartanī kiraṇasaṃhatirēva śaktā
dharmaṃ nijaṃ śamayituṃ nijayaiva vṛṣṭyā ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Īśā, it is you alone who can remove the entire burning heat of saṃsāra of bound souls — since all of it is under your control — through your compassion alone. Like the sun dispersing shadow, your mere glance removes the darkness.
contentBlocks.meanings: hartum tvam eva bhavasi = it is you alone who can be (bhavasi) the remover (hartum = to take away); tvad-adhīnam = under your control (tvad = your; adhīna = dependent on, controlled by); īśe = O Ruler/Mistress; saṃsāra-tāpam akhilam = the entire (akhila) burning heat (tāpa) of saṃsāra; dayayā = through compassion (dayā); paśūnām = of the bound ones/creatures (paśu = one bound by the three malas in Śaiva thought); vaikartanī kiraṇa... (implied): like the sun's rays (vaikartana = the sun) removing shadow.

contentBlocks.original:
शक्तिः शरीरमधिदैवतमन्तरात्मा
ज्ञानं क्रिया करणमासनजालमिच्छा ।
ऐश्वर्यमायतनमावरणानि च त्वं
किं तन्न यद्भवसि देवि शशाङ्कमौलेः ॥ २५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śaktiḥ śarīramadhidaivatamantarātmā
jñānaṃ kriyā karaṇamāsanajālamichChā ।
aiśvaryamāyatanamāvaraṇāni cha tvaṃ
kiṃ tanna yadbhavasi dēvi śaśāṅkamaulēḥ ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Śakti, body, the overlord-deity, the inner self, knowledge, action, the instruments of perception and action, the network of āsanas, will — you are all of these, O Amba. What in the universe is not you?
contentBlocks.meanings: śaktiḥ = power/energy (the fundamental Śākta principle); śarīram = the body; adhidaivata = the overlord-deity (the presiding cosmic intelligence); antara-ātmā = the inner self (the witness-consciousness); jñānaṃ = knowledge; kriyā = action; karaṇam = the instruments (the five jñānendriyas and five karmendriyas); āsana-jālam = the network (jāla) of āsanas (postures — possibly the thirty-two or sixty-four āsanas of the yogic tradition, each identified with a form of the Goddess); icchā = will/desire (the first of the three Śāktopāya states: icchā-jñāna-kriyā).

contentBlocks.original:
भूमौ निवृत्तिरुदिता पयसि प्रतिष्ठा
विद्याऽनले मरुति शान्तिरतीवकान्तिः ।
व्योम्नीति याः किल कलाः कलयन्ति विश्वं
तासां हि दूरतरमम्ब पदं त्वदीयम् ॥ २६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhūmau nivṛttiruditā payasi pratiṣṭhā
vidyā'nalē maruti śāntiratīvakāntiḥ ।
vyōmnīti yāḥ kila kalāḥ kalayanti viśvaṃ
tāsāṃ hi dūrataramamba padaṃ tvadīyam ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Nivṛtti is born in earth, Pratiṣṭhā in water, Vidyā in fire, Śānti in wind — you alone are all these energies, the kalās of the five paths, O Amba.
contentBlocks.meanings: bhūmau nivṛttiḥ uditā = Nivṛtti arisen (uditā) in earth (bhūmi) — Nivṛtti is the first of the five kalā-śaktis (power-aspects): withdrawal/cessation; payasi pratiṣṭhā = Pratiṣṭhā in water (payasi = in water) — establishment/grounding; vidyā anale = Vidyā in fire (anala) — knowledge/illumination; maruti śāntiḥ = Śānti in wind (marut) — peace/tranquility; atīva-kāntiḥ (and implied Śāntyatītā in space) = exceeding-beauty, the fifth kalā beyond the five elements.

contentBlocks.original:
यावत्पदं पदसरोजयुगं त्वदीयं
नाङ्गीकरोति हृदयेषु जगच्छरण्ये ।
तावद्विकल्पजटिलाः कुटिलप्रकारा-
-स्तर्कग्रहाः समयिनां प्रलयं न यान्ति ॥ २७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yāvatpadaṃ padasarōjayugaṃ tvadīyaṃ
nāṅgīkarōti hṛdayēṣu jagachCharaṇyē ।
tāvadvikalpajaṭilāḥ kuṭilaprakārā-
-starkagrahāḥ samayināṃ pralayaṃ na yānti ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O refuge of the world, as long as the pair of your lotus-feet is not welcomed in the heart — so long do the thickets of false constructs flourish, and the darkness of delusion does not lift.
contentBlocks.meanings: yāvat = as long as; padam pada-saroja-yugaṃ tvadīyam = the place of (padam) your (tvadīyam) pair of lotus-feet (pāda-saroja-yuga); nāṅgīkaroti = is not accepted/welcomed (na + āṅgīkaroti = does not embrace); hṛdayeṣu = in the hearts; jagac-charaṇye = O refuge of the world (jagat = world; śaraṇya = shelter/refuge); tāvad vikalpajaTa... = so long (tāvat) the thicket (jaṭā) of false constructs (vikalpa — the discursive, dividing activity of the mind).

contentBlocks.original:
निर्देवयानपितृयानविहारमेके
कृत्वा मनः करणमण्डलसार्वभौमम् ।
ध्याने निवेश्य तव कारणपञ्चकस्य
पर्वाणि पार्वति नयन्ति निजासनत्वम् ॥ २८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nirdēvayānapitṛyānavihāramēkē
kṛtvā manaḥ karaṇamaṇḍalasārvabhaumam ।
dhyānē nivēśya tava kāraṇapañchakasya
parvāṇi pārvati nayanti nijāsanatvam ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Some, having made the mind sovereign over the entire circle of faculties, freed from wandering through the paths of the gods and ancestors — fixing it in meditation — reach the ground of your being in this very body.
contentBlocks.meanings: nir-deva-yāna-pitṛ-yāna-vihāram = freed from (nis = without) the wandering (vihāra) through the path of the gods (deva-yāna) and the path of the ancestors (pitṛ-yāna) — the two post-death paths of the Chāndogya Upaniṣad: rebirth and liberation; ekam = singularly; kṛtvā = having made; manaḥ karaṇa-maṇḍala-sārvabhaumam = the mind (manas) sovereign (sārvabhauma = universal emperor) over the sphere/circle (maṇḍala) of sense-faculties (karaṇa); dhyāne niveśya = having fixed (niveśya) in meditation (dhyāna).

contentBlocks.original:
स्थूलासु मूर्तिषु महीप्रमुखासु मूर्तेः
कस्याश्चनापि तव वैभवमम्ब यस्याः ।
पत्या गिरामपि न शक्यत एव वक्तुं
सापि स्तुता किल मयेति तितिक्षितव्यम् ॥ २९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sthūlāsu mūrtiṣu mahīpramukhāsu mūrtēḥ
kasyāśchanāpi tava vaibhavamamba yasyāḥ ।
patyā girāmapi na śakyata ēva vaktuṃ
sāpi stutā kila mayēti titikṣitavyam ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Amba, in any of the gross forms — earth and the rest — no one can measure the greatness of your manifestation. How much less can the subtler forms and the supreme formless reality be described?
contentBlocks.meanings: sthūlāsu mūrtiṣu = in the gross (sthūla) forms (mūrti); mahī-pramukhāsu = beginning with (pramukha = beginning with, foremost) the earth (mahī); mūrteḥ kasyāś canāpi = of any (kasyāś can = whichever) form (mūrti); tava vaibhavam = your greatness/power (vaibhava); amba = O Amba; yasyāḥ = of which; implied: no one can measure even the gross level — how much more the subtle and the supreme.

contentBlocks.original:
कालाग्निकोटिरुचिमम्ब षडध्वशुद्धौ
आप्लावनेषु भवतीममृतौघवृष्टिम् ।
श्यामां घनस्तनतटां शकलीकृताघां
ध्यायन्त एव जगतां गुरवो भवन्ति ॥ ३० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kālāgnikōṭiruchimamba ṣaḍadhvaśuddhau
āplāvanēṣu bhavatīmamṛtaughavṛṣṭim ।
śyāmāṃ ghanastanataṭāṃ śakalīkṛtāghāṃ
dhyāyanta ēva jagatāṃ guravō bhavanti ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Amba, blazing with the brilliance of ten million fires of dissolution — in the purifications of the six paths (ṣaḍadhvan), in the flooding-consecrations, devotees experience you as a shower of nectar-torrents. Such is the paradox of your nature: fire and amṛta in one.
contentBlocks.meanings: kālāgni-koṭi-rucim = possessing the brilliance (ruci) of ten million (koṭi) fires of time/dissolution (kālāgni = the fire of time, the eschatological fire that burns all at the end of an age); amba = O Amba; ṣaḍ-adhva-śuddhau = in the purification (śuddhi) of the six paths (ṣaḍ-adhvan — the six ways of manifestation in Trika Śaivism: mantra, pada, varṇa, kalā, tattva, bhuvana); āplāvaneṣu = in the flooding/inundation ceremonies (āplāvana = soaking, consecration rites); bhavatīm amṛta-ogha-vṛṣṭim = [they experience] you as a rain (vṛṣṭi) of torrents (ogha) of amṛta.

contentBlocks.original:
विद्यां परां कतिचिदम्बरमम्ब केचि-
-दानन्दमेव कतिचित्कतिचिच्च मायाम् ।
त्वां विश्वमाहुरपरे वयमामनामः
साक्षादपारकरुणां गुरुमूर्तिमेव ॥ ३१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vidyāṃ parāṃ katichidambaramamba kēchi-
-dānandamēva katichitkatichichcha māyām ।
tvāṃ viśvamāhuraparē vayamāmanāmaḥ
sākṣādapārakaruṇāṃ gurumūrtimēva ॥ 31 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Amba, some call you the Supreme Vidyā, some call you Ambara (the sky), some say you are pure Ānanda alone, and some call you Māyā. But you transcend all categories, known only to yourself.
contentBlocks.meanings: vidyāṃ parāṃ = the supreme (parā) Vidyā (knowledge — the liberating light of consciousness); katicit = some; ambaraṃ = sky/ether (ākāśa — the subtlest element and the ground of all); amba = O Amba; kecit = some; ānandam eva = pure Ānanda (bliss — the third of sat-cit-ānanda); katicit = some; katicic ca māyām = some [call you] Māyā; tvām... = you [are known only to yourself / transcend all these names].

contentBlocks.original:
कुवलयदलनीलं बर्बरस्निग्धकेशं
पृथुतरकुचभाराक्रान्तकान्तावलग्नम् ।
किमिह बहुभिरुक्तैस्त्वत्स्वरूपं परं नः
सकलजननि मातः सन्ततं सन्निधत्ताम् ॥ ३२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kuvalayadalanīlaṃ barbarasnigdhakēśaṃ
pṛthutarakuchabhārākrāntakāntāvalagnam ।
kimiha bahubhiruktaistvatsvarūpaṃ paraṃ naḥ
sakalajanani mātaḥ santataṃ sannidhattām ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Blue as the petal of a blue lotus, with wavy, well-oiled tresses, her slender waist compressed under the weight of full breasts — on this form the meditative mind rests. There is no other vision comparable.
contentBlocks.meanings: kuvalaya-dala-nīlam = blue (nīla) as a blue-lotus (kuvalaya) petal (dala); barbara-snigdha-keśam = with wavy (barbara = curly/tribal) oiled/sleek (snigdha) hair (keśa); pṛthutara-kuca-bhārākrānta-kāntā-valamgnam = a slender (kānta = beloved/lovely) waist compressed (āvalagna = clinging tightly) and overwhelmed (ākrānta) by the weight (bhāra) of the very full (pṛthutara) breasts (kuca); kim iha bahu = what more (is there) to say.

contentBlocks.original: इति श्रीकालिदास विरचित पञ्चस्तव्यां चतुर्थः अम्बास्तवः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti śrīkāḻidāsa virachita pañchastavyāṃ chaturthaḥ ambāstavaḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the Amba Stava, the fourth hymn in the Pañchastavya composed by Śrī Kālidāsa.
contentBlocks.meanings: iti = thus (standard closing marker); śrī-kālidāsa viracita = composed (viracita) by Śrī Kālidāsa; pañchastavyāṃ = in the Pañchastavya (the collection of five hymns); caturthaḥ = the fourth; ambā-stavaḥ = the Amba-stava (hymn to Ambā).
